# Extended Case References — Lecture mKFI3E26KTU

*Brief mentions of canonical cases that did not warrant a full `case_index.md` entry. Generated by reconciliation pass against the 1,676-cluster canon. Substantive treatments are in `case_index.md`.*

## Cases mentioned in passing

### US-Japan rare earth metals embargo
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "US-Japan rare earth metals embargo"
- **Anchor:** `§2.p8`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "China has exported at very low prices for a very long time, making it difficult for the rest of the world to produce. One of the best mines in the world, in southern California, for light rare earths actually shut down in 2002 due to Chinese competition. Then in 2010 — does anyone know what happened to the rare earth market then? Yes, they stopped selling to Japan, because of a border dispute. So suddenly there was panic throughout the world. Fortunately Japan was well prepared because they had the analysis of the critical materials. Toru Okabe, who was a postdoc in Don Sadoway's lab years..."
